recap: England survived a perfect storm of adversity to beat Mexico 3-2 in an enthralling last-16 encounter, with Jude Bellingham's two early headers giving them control before Jarell Quansah's red card and Julian Quinones' goal made it a desperate rear-guard action at altitude. Harry Kane's penalty restored their two-goal lead after the dismissal, and despite Raul Jimenez pulling another back from the spot, Thomas Tuchel's side held firm to set up a quarter-final against Norway. Jordan Pickford's world-class saves and a collective defensive effort proved decisive on a chaotic night in Mexico City.
